reasoning: Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl' debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 in October 2025 with 4 million units and retained the #1 spot through its 3rd week (194k units). While incredibly successful, securing 17 consecutive weeks at #1 is virtually impossible in the current streaming era. Swift's own career best is 12 consecutive weeks (set by 'The Tortured Poets Department'), meaning this would have to surpass her all-time record. Furthermore, a 17-week streak run starting in late October 2025 would extend through February 2026, forcing the album to outcompete standard high-profile fall releases as well as the inevitable, massive streaming surge of holiday albums in December that typically top the charts. Given the steep drop in album-equivalent units (from 4 million to 194k by Week 3) and historical Billboard context, this will almost certainly resolve to NO. I am submitting the minimum allowed probability of 5%.
